beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, light breakfast, check weather and knitting project.
beats.reasoning: Full energy start to the day; takes time to ease in with coffee and check conditions before heading out. Madrona is scenic and residential, fitting his frugal nature.

beats.activity: Drive from Madrona through scenic route to Sea-Tac, avoiding Light Rail closure impact.
beats.reasoning: Raj works as an air traffic controller at Sea-Tac. Leaves early enough to take a scenic drive and avoid morning traffic. Light Rail closure doesn't affect his drive route.

beats.activity: Work shift at air traffic control tower, monitoring early morning flights.
beats.reasoning: Morning shift work before the FIFA match airs; Raj is professional and focused during work hours, so he won't be distracted by the 11am USA vs Wales match.

beats.activity: Short break, grab lunch from airport café, skip the FIFA match—not a crowd person.
beats.reasoning: Lunch break at work. The FIFA match is airing at 11am downtown, but Raj is skeptical of crowds and values quiet time; he stays at work rather than rushing to a bar.

beats.activity: Continue afternoon work shift, managing mid-day and early evening traffic.
beats.reasoning: Full afternoon shift as ATC; maintains focus on the job. Sunny weather makes for good flying conditions and steady traffic.

beats.activity: Drive from Sea-Tac back to Madrona via scenic route along the water.
beats.reasoning: End of shift. Takes the scenic route home as is his habit, enjoying the sunny June evening and water views. Avoids the Light Rail closure area entirely.

beats.activity: Errand: pop into QFC for groceries and supplies, then head home.
beats.reasoning: Quick neighborhood errand while still in good energy; frugal nature means he shops locally. Sunny weather makes the walk pleasant.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner at home: simple pasta and salad.
beats.reasoning: Home-cooked meal fits his frugal lifestyle and gives him time to unwind after work. Madrona is quiet, away from downtown crowds.

beats.activity: Work on knitting project by the window, enjoying the long June evening light.; Paint or sketch, or read; wind down with tea.
beats.reasoning: Knitting is his main creative outlet; the sunny weather and long daylight make this an ideal time. Quiet, meditative activity that suits his personality.

beats.activity: Evening routine: shower, prepare for bed, wind down.
beats.reasoning: Final wind-down at home before sleep. Stays well before the 23:30 cutoff for non-sleep beats.
Full 7.5-hour sleep cycle, ending well before 06:00 threshold. Overnight beat spans into the next calendar day as required.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather—coffee tasted better knowing the day would be sunny. The drive to Sea-Tac along the water was quiet this morning; everyone else was probably heading downtown for that USA-Wales match. The tower was steady, which I needed. Grabbed a sandwich at the café around 10:30 and deliberately avoided the bars. Not my scene, especially on a Monday. The afternoon flights kept me focused, which is exactly where I like to be. On the drive back, I took the scenic route again—something about the water in June light just settles me. Quick stop at QFC, then home to pasta and the knitting project. Spent the evening by the window with my needles, watching the light linger. Made a cup of tea and just... sat. Feeling calm. Grateful for days like this.
thought_process: I chose to skip the FIFA crowd entirely—that's just not who I am. Being around that energy, even at an airport café, would've drained me. My work requires sharp focus and calm, and I protect that by staying away from chaos, even fun chaos. The Light Rail closure didn't really affect me since I drive the scenic route anyway; I've learned Seattle well enough to know my own paths. The long June evening pulled me toward the knitting—something tactile and meditative after hours of radio communication and constant vigilance. I think the sunny weather lifted something in me, made the day feel less like routine and more like a gift. By evening I was genuinely content, not just tired.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
